title: Highlight active file in left sidebar when tab is focused
description: When a tab is active and present in the sidebar tree, visually highlight the matching sidebar item.
notes: Implemented sidebar active-file highlighting for tree/recent/timeline views using a shared path-matching helper; synced highlight on tab focus switches, folder expansion, and save-as path updates. Added node:test coverage in test/sidebar-highlight.test.js and verified with npm test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add advanced global search controls (match case, regex, include/exclude, replace)
description: Current global search supports plain text matches but lacks common VS Code controls. Add UX controls for match case, regex mode, include/exclude globs, and replace across results to reduce context switching to external editors.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Global search UI includes toggles for Match Case and Regex. 2) Include/Exclude pattern inputs are available and applied during search. 3) Results can be replaced per-match and per-file with undo confirmation. 4) Keyboard flow remains usable (Cmd+Shift+F opens, Enter/arrow navigation still works).

title: Add spacing between sibling heading sections
description: Consecutive h2 sections (like Topics, Organizations, People) appear cramped with insufficient vertical spacing between them. The CSS removes margin-top from headings inside .md-section containers but doesn't add spacing between sibling sections. Fix: Add margin-top to .md-section + .md-section selector to create proper breathing room between sections.
close_reason: Added .md-section + .md-section { margin-top: 24px } for proper spacing

title: Quick Open URL support
description: Support pasting URLs into the Quick Open (Cmd+P) palette to quickly open them in the default browser.
close_reason: Closed

title: Detect and reconcile on-disk renames/moves
description: If a file is renamed on disk outside the app, detect it and update open tabs/sidebar references without breaking state.
notes: Implemented external rename/move reconciliation using inode-based detection in file watchers; emit file-path-changed and update renderer tab/sidebar references to prevent broken state. Added unit tests for rename and move detection in file-watch-utils.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reveal folder in tree from recent view
description: Clicking on a folder header in the 'Recent Changes' view should switch the sidebar back to the standard 'Tree View', expand the clicked folder, and scroll it into view.
close_reason: Closed

title: Live filesystem watch for left sidebar
description: Left sidebar should watch filesystem changes and refresh live for add/remove/rename/move events.
close_reason: Added live sidebar filesystem refresh watcher that auto-reloads tree on external add/remove/rename changes.

title: Chat parsing for terminal view
description:
Layer on top of Terminal View:
- Detect Human:/Assistant: patterns
- Visual separation between conversation turns
- Collapsible sections for tool calls
- Works with pasted terminal output and structured JSONL
close_reason: Closed

title: Improve wiki link path resolution (support folder paths)
description:
Wiki links with folder paths aren't resolving correctly.

**Example that failed:**
[[topics/Sussman_s_paper_on_controlled_hallucinations|Sussman's paper on controlled hallucinations]]

**Expected behavior:**
- Support [[folder/filename|display]] syntax
- Match by relative path from folder root
- Fall back to filename-only match if path doesn't match

**Also add:**
- Hover on opened folder path shows full path
- Right-click on folder path to copy path
close_reason: Closed

title: Right-click in content area shows tab context menu
description: Right-clicking in the content area should show the same context menu as right-clicking the tab. This eliminates the need to find the active tab when you want to close, duplicate, rename, or perform other tab actions. The user is already focused on the content, so the context menu appears where their attention is. Simple and discoverable.
close_reason: Implemented: right-click in markdown content area now shows tab context menu

title: Add document comments in view mode
Show upload success message after publishing
close_reason: Implemented toast notifications for publish success/error

title: Terminal display mode with optional chat parsing
description:
## Core Feature: Terminal View
A display mode optimized for terminal/CLI content:
- **Monospace font** throughout
- **Dark background** (terminal aesthetic)
- **Preserved whitespace** and formatting
- **No markdown rendering** - shows content as-is

## Use Cases
- Saved terminal sessions
- Log files
- Build output
- AI chat transcripts (Claude Code, etc.)
- Any CLI output

## View Menu
```
View
├── ✓ Standard View
├── Terminal View       ⌘⇧T
```

## Optional Enhancement: Chat Parsing
When enabled (or auto-detected), layer on:
- Visual separation between Human/Assistant turns
- Collapsible sections for tool calls
- But the base terminal view works without any parsing

## Technical Approach
1. Add CSS class for terminal mode (monospace, dark bg, preserve whitespace)
2. View menu toggle with keyboard shortcut
3. (Later) Add heuristic chat parser as enhancement
4. (Later) Add JSONL parser for Claude Code sessions

title: Global search within opened folder (VS Code style)
description:
Add a global search feature when a folder is opened, similar to VS Code's Cmd+Shift+F:

**Core features:**
- Search across all files in the opened folder
- Show results grouped by file with line previews
- Click result → open file and jump to that line
- Highlight matches in the preview

**UI options:**
- Sidebar panel (like VS Code)
- Or modal search overlay (like Cmd+P in VS Code)
- Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Shift+F

**Search options (nice to have):**
- Case sensitive toggle
- Regex support
- File type filters (*.md only, etc.)
- Include/exclude patterns

**Performance:**
- Index files on folder open
- Debounce search input
- Virtual scroll for large result sets
close_reason: Implemented global search with Cmd+Shift+F: search across all files in folder, grouped results, keyboard navigation, match highlighting

title: Review settings vs View menu placement for layout controls
description: Audit whether content margin/layout controls belong in Settings or View menu; consolidate for clearer IA.

title: Add dev mode restart button
description: Add a feature to detect source code changes in dev mode and show a restart button in the titlebar.

title: Preserve scroll position when switching between edit and preview modes
description: When switching from preview to edit mode (or vice versa), the view jumps to the top. Should preserve scroll position using percentage-based or content-matching approach.

title: Drag and drop files into folders in left sidebar
description: Enable drag-and-drop reorganization of files into folders in the left sidebar.
notes: Implemented sidebar file->folder drag and drop with validated move-file-to-directory IPC, tree state sync, open-tab path updates, and new unit tests for file move safety + tree move behavior.
close_reason: Implemented drag-and-drop move of files into folders in the sidebar tree with move validation.

title: Standardize rename UX and fix double-click rename bug

close_reason: Standardized rename entry points by adding Rename actions in tab/sidebar context menus and wiring to rename flows.

title: EasyMDE split mode: toolbar disappears and can't close split view
description: When opening side-by-side split mode in EasyMDE rich editor, the toolbar disappears and there's no way to close the split view.

title: Create new file directly inside selected folder from left sidebar
description: Support creating a new file in the currently selected folder from left sidebar context.
notes: Implemented selected-folder file creation from sidebar tree: right-click folder -> New File Here, plus command palette path (Sidebar: New File in Selected Folder). Added selected folder state + nested temp file insertion/rename flow and case-insensitive collision utility tests.
close_reason: Completed: create-new-file in selected sidebar folder with context menu + command path, collision validation coverage, and tests.

title: Default to edit mode when creating new file
close_reason: Implemented in this session

title: Fix Command-Shift-S shortcut handling
description: Cmd+Shift+S is currently not working; shortcut should trigger expected Save As / mapped action.

title: Clarify sidebar sort mode/status in UI
description: Make current sidebar sort mode obvious (label/tooltip/indicator) and improve legibility so users understand what ordering is active.
notes: Implemented clear sidebar sort status with persistent label + indicator + tooltip. Added shared helper (sidebar-sort-indicator.js), wired renderer sort UI updates via updateSidebarSortUI(), updated sidebar header markup/styles for legible status pill, and added node tests in tests/sidebar-sort-indicator.test.js. Verified with npm test (all passing).
close_reason: Implemented and tested sort mode status indicator UI

title: Markdown diff and track-changes mode for writing
description: Add a writing workflow with diffs/track changes similar to editor review flows (VS Code-style compare/inline change visibility).

title: Fix Command-F input losing focus after first character
description: Find box/search input currently drops focus after typing one character.
close_reason: Fixed find bar focus loss by preserving input focus during live match updates in editor search flow.

title: Research UX gaps vs VS Code and propose parity roadmap
description: Audit markdown-reader against standard VS Code behaviors (navigation, sidebar, tabs, search, rename/move, context menus, split editors, update flow). Deliver prioritized gaps with concrete acceptance criteria and map to existing/new bd tickets.
close_reason: Added VS Code parity gap analysis and research notes with prioritized mapping to tickets.

title: Fix publish error - handle 'File storage not configured' gracefully with helpful link

Current margin settings are limited. Want:

2. Make 'Spacious' (widest) setting even wider - less margin, more content width
3. Possibly add preset options: Compact, Normal, Spacious, Full-width

title: Open in Finder action for files/folders across app
description: Provide Open in Finder from left sidebar, tab bar, and other relevant file context menus.
close_reason: Added Open in Finder/Revel in Finder actions across tab, sidebar file/folder, and folder-path context menus.

title: Resizable left sidebar via drag handle
description: Allow the left sidebar width to be expanded/collapsed by dragging, similar to standard editors.
close_reason: Implemented draggable sidebar resizer with constrained width and session persistence.

title: Add more App Store screenshots
description: Consider adding additional screenshots to the Mac App Store listing. Current: 1 screenshot (1440x900 dark mode with features list). Could add: light mode, editing view, multiple tabs, file browser sidebar.

title: Command-F search not working in edit mode

title: Auto-increment build number on every build

title: kljlj
Unify content width and margins into single custom setting
description:
## Current State
- Content Width: presets only (700, 900, 1100, 1300, 1500, 1800, full)
- Content Margins: presets only (8, 16, 20, 28, 40px padding)
- No way to set arbitrary values

## Proposed
Consider unifying into a single 'Content Width' setting that:
- Allows arbitrary pixel values (input field or slider)
- Or maybe just add 'Custom...' option that opens input dialog
- The margin/padding setting may be redundant if width handles it

## Questions
- Should margins be separate from width?
- What's the UX for custom values? Submenu with input? Separate dialog?
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix back button navigation to previous tab/history item
description: Back button should navigate to previous tab/history entry reliably; currently not working as expected.
notes: Reproduced regression by tracing tab switch flow: renderer.js has two switchToTab implementations, and the later EasyMDE override (active at runtime) did not call pushNavHistory(). As a result, tab switches stopped recording history, so Back could not navigate to prior tab/history entries. Fixed by restoring pushNavHistory(tabId, tab.filePath) in the EasyMDE switchToTab override before updateTabUI(). Targeted validation: rg confirmed pushNavHistory present in both switchToTab paths; node --check renderer.js passed.
close_reason: Fixed back/forward reliability by ensuring tab switches push consistent navigation history entries.

title: Show full path tooltip for tabs and left sidebar items
description: Hovering a file item in top tab bar or left sidebar should show full path tooltip.
notes: Verified full-path tooltips on top tabs and left sidebar file items (tree/recent/timeline). Added tab-title tooltip propagation and ensured sidebar file elements expose full path via title attribute.
close_reason: Closed

title: Menu item for update status and manual check/apply update
description: Add explicit update status and update action in the app menu, complementing passive update indicator.
close_reason: Added update status indicator and manual Check for Updates action in app menu with available/download state.

title: Add scroll wheel navigation for tabs
description: Allow two-finger scroll (trackpad) or scroll wheel to navigate through tabs in the tab bar. Idiomatic approach: Listen for wheel events on .tab-bar and scroll horizontally when tabs overflow. This matches behavior in Chrome, VS Code, and other macOS apps with tab bars.
close_reason: Added wheel event listener to convert vertical scroll to horizontal tab bar scroll

title: Timeline view for recent changes
description: Add a new view mode to the 'Recent Changes' feature that groups modified files by date (Today, Yesterday, Previous 7 Days) and then by parent folder. This provides a timeline-based perspective on work.

title: Show recently modified files in the current open folder
description: Design and implement a feature to view recently modified files in the currently open folder, sorted by recency. This helps users quickly identify which files have been updated.

title: Add VS Code-style split editor view (side-by-side tabs)
description: Add split editor workflow to view/edit two files side-by-side (or split same file), with tab-to-split actions and resizeable divider similar to standard editors.

title: Add breadcrumb path bar for active file navigation
description: Standard editors expose active-file location with clickable breadcrumbs. Add a breadcrumb bar above content showing workspace-relative path segments and current heading/file context to reduce navigation friction in nested folders.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Breadcrumbs show workspace root -> folder segments -> file name for active tab. 2) Each folder segment is clickable and reveals that folder in sidebar. 3) Breadcrumbs update on tab switch and file rename/move. 4) Hidden/overflow handling works on small window widths.

title: EasyMDE preview doesn't render lists with dashes as bullets
description: In split/side-by-side mode, dashes (-) aren't being rendered as bullet points. This is NOT intentional - EasyMDE is using its own bundled markdown parser instead of the app's configured marked.js with proper rendering. Fix: Add previewRender option to EasyMDE config that uses the app's marked instance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Obsidian markdown support
Auto-parse AI chat transcripts into formatted sections
description:
When pasting AI chat history (from Clawd, Claude, etc.), automatically parse and format it:
- Detect chat patterns (User:/Assistant:, Human:/Claude:, etc.)
- Convert to formatted sections with headings or visual distinction
- Make conversations more readable in markdown view
- Could be auto-detect on paste or a manual 'Format as Chat' action

Visual distinction:
- Clear visual difference between human messages and AI messages
- Different styling/colors/backgrounds for each speaker
- Appropriate margins/spacing between messages for readability

Collapse functionality:
- Consider 'Collapse all AI responses' button
- Or leverage existing section collapse features
- Evaluate if current heading collapse covers this use case or if dedicated chat collapse is needed

Terminal formatting preservation:
- Research: Can we preserve/import terminal formatting (ANSI colors, syntax highlighting)?
- Export option to retain terminal colors/highlights from CLI tools
- Consider parsing ANSI escape codes and converting to styled HTML/markdown
- Useful for Claude Code transcripts, other CLI chat tools

Layout/Margins:
- Chat-specific margin settings (tighter or looser spacing between turns)
- Consider chat bubble style vs linear style options
- Respect global margin settings but allow chat-specific overrides

title: Sort files by recency in sidebar
description: Add a sort toggle button to the sidebar header to switch between 'Name' (A-Z) and 'Date Modified' (Newest First) sorting for the standard file tree view.
close_reason: Closed

title: Formalize frontend log forwarding
description: Ensure frontend console logs are always forwarded to the main process terminal in development mode for easier debugging. Currently implemented ad-hoc in renderer.js.
close_reason: Closed

title: Consider full WYSIWYG editing mode
Support Obsidian-style wiki links within opened folders
description:
When a folder is opened in OpenMarkdownReader, support Obsidian-style [[wiki links]]:

**Behavior:**
- Parse `[[page name]]` syntax in markdown files
- Resolve links by matching against filenames in the opened folder (without .md extension)
- Click on wiki link → open that file in a new tab (or switch to existing tab)

**Conflict handling:**
- If multiple files have the same name (in different subdirectories), choose the first match
- Show a warning/toast when a conflict is detected
- Could potentially show disambiguation UI in future

**Requirements:**
- Folder must be opened (not just a single file)
- Links are relative to the folder root
- Support both `[[page]]` and `[[page|display text]]` syntax

**Nice to have:**
- Backlinks panel showing what links to the current page
- Broken link highlighting (links to non-existent pages)
close_reason: Implemented Obsidian-style wiki links: [[page]] and [[page|alias]] syntax, conflict warnings, broken link styling

title: Make pasting terminal output friendly
WYSIWYG edit mode that renders markdown inline
description: Option to have edit mode render markdown inline like the preview, similar to Typora or Obsidian live preview. Could use existing EasyMDE preview features or integrate a WYSIWYG library like Milkdown/Tiptap.

comments.author: jacobcole
comments.text:
## Research Notes

**Options for WYSIWYG/live-preview edit mode:**

| Approach | Raw Text Visible | Rendered Inline | Effort |
|----------|------------------|-----------------|--------|
| EasyMDE (current) | Yes | No | N/A |
| Milkdown/Tiptap | No | Yes (pure WYSIWYG) | Medium |
| Obsidian-style (CodeMirror 6) | Yes | Yes (decorations) | High |
| Split pane (EasyMDE built-in) | Yes | Side-by-side | Already available |

**Recommendations:**
- Milkdown is pure WYSIWYG - hides raw markdown, shows rendered output only
- Obsidian approach is best of both worlds (see raw syntax but styled) - requires CodeMirror 6 with custom decorations
- EasyMDE already has side-by-side mode (columns icon in Rich toolbar)

**Libraries to consider:**
- Milkdown (ProseMirror-based, ~40kb gzipped)
- Tiptap (ProseMirror-based, very flexible)
- CodeMirror 6 with markdown decorations (what Obsidian uses)

title: RTF with formatting support
Show word count for current document
description: Add visible word count for the active markdown document.
notes: Implemented a titlebar word-count indicator for the active document. Added shared countWords utility (Unicode-aware, handles contractions/hyphenation), wired live updates for preview/plain/rich edit flows, and added node tests in tests/word-count.test.js.
close_reason: Completed: current-document word count visible in UI with tests.

title: Daily note opens directly in edit mode
description: Creating a new daily note should immediately enter edit mode instead of preview/read mode.
notes: Reproduced by tracing daily-note flow: createDailyNote() writes new file, then calls loadMarkdownFile() without forceEdit, so renderer opens in preview/read mode. Implemented fix in main.js: track newly created file and call loadMarkdownFile(win, filePath, { forceEdit: createdNewFile }). Existing daily notes still open in current mode; only newly created notes force edit mode. Verification: node --check main.js passed.
close_reason: Daily note creation now opens newly created note directly in edit mode.

title: Add one-click publish to noos (globalbr.ai)
description: Add publish button that uploads current file to noos anonymously and copies shareable URL to clipboard
close_reason: Implemented publish button that uploads to noos anonymous endpoint

title: Add back/forward navigation (browser-style history)
description:
Add browser-style back/forward navigation for file history:

**Features:**
- Back button (Cmd+[) to go to previous file/location
- Forward button (Cmd+]) to go forward after going back
- Navigation history stack tracking opened files
- Buttons in titlebar or toolbar

**Behavior:**
- Opening a file pushes to history
- Back navigates to previous file
- Forward only available after using back
- History clears forward stack on new navigation
close_reason: Implemented back/forward navigation with Cmd+[ and Cmd+], buttons in titlebar, Go menu

title: JSONL parser for Claude Code sessions
description:
Parse Claude Code session files (~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl):
- Auto-detect format on file open
- Extract human/assistant/tool messages
- Render with Terminal View + chat parsing
- Show timestamps, thinking blocks (toggleable)
close_reason: Closed

Fix Publish button: CSP, icon direction, size

title: Fix heading collapse arrow wrapping
description: On narrow screens, the collapse arrow (triangle) for headings wraps to the line above the heading text. It should remain inline with the heading using proper flexbox or white-space handling.
